quote.text: There are two ways to write error-free programs; only the third one works
quote.author: Alan J. Perlis

links.title: Cracking JWTs
links.description: **ticarpi/jwt_tool** is a toolkit for testing, tweaking and cracking JSON Web Tokens. If you like JWT tokens, you can definitely learn a lot by just looking at the guides in this repository!

links.title: Compiler Compiler
links.description: "Compiler Compiler" is an incredible video series about building a JavaScript engine. Created by a Mozilla engineer and based on SpiderMonkey, Mozilla's JavaScript engine.

links.title: The Return of the 90s Web
links.description: Are we ready to revisit some of the ideas of the early web again? There are trends that suggest we might just have come full circle - and I like it.

links.title: How to use React Testing Library
links.description: Learn how to use React Testing Library in this tutorial. You will learn how to test your React components step by step with unit and integration tests.

links.title: How one word in PostgreSQL unlocked a 9x performance improvement
links.description: At the very heart of Actual is a custom syncing engine. I'd like to talk about a PostgreSQL feature that enabled a 9-10x performance improvement.

links.title: Online text to diagram tools
links.description: A long list of text to diagram tools. Next time you will have to generate a diagram, you should try one of these tools. What are the advantages? Well, just to name a few, you can keep your diagrams on git as text and you will always have a consistent graphic!

links.title: Five common mistakes writing react components (with hooks) in 2020
links.description: The most common mistakes I found writing react components, why they are mistakes and how to avoid or fix them.

book.title: Angular 2: From Theory To Practice
book.author: Asim Hussain
book.description: In this quickstart you'll get a 50,000 foot view of the major features of Angular 2 including: Typescript & ES6 Javascript, Components & Binding, Directives, Dependancy Injection & Services, Angular Modules & Bootstrapping your Angular application., SPAs & Routing, Angular CLI, Forms, Reactive Programming with RXJs, HTTP and finally Unit Testing.
